description: Add comprehensive E2E tests covering the plugin and browser tools menu version items: icon selection (Package vs FolderOpen), click behavior (npm page vs open folder), the isNpmInstall heuristic, the destructive item border separator, and the remove/uninstall label difference based on plugin source.

userStories.title: E2E tests for local plugin version menu item
userStories.description: Add E2E tests verifying the version item in the three-dot menu for local plugins: FolderOpen icon is shown (not Package), version text matches, the item is enabled (not disabled), and the remove item says 'Remove' (not 'Uninstall'). Also verify the destructive remove item has a top border separator.
acceptanceCriteria: Test opens the three-dot menu on the local e2e-test plugin card and verifies a menuitem containing the version string (e.g. 'v0.0.66') is visible
Test verifies the version menuitem contains a lucide FolderOpen SVG icon (not Package) by checking for the SVG path content or data attribute
Test verifies the version menuitem is not disabled (no data-disabled attribute)
Test verifies the Remove menuitem text is 'Remove' (not 'Uninstall') for local plugins
Test verifies the destructive Remove menuitem has a visible top border (border-t class or computed style)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-menu-version.e2e.ts (new file)

Context:
The e2e-test plugin is always registered as a local plugin via `localPlugins` in the test config (fixtures.ts:230-246). Its source is always `'local'` and it has a `sourcePath` pointing to the plugin's absolute directory.

The VersionItem component in PluginMenu.tsx renders differently based on `plugin.source`:
- `source === 'npm'`: `<Package>` icon + version, click opens npmjs.com
- `source === 'local'` with sourcePath: `<FolderOpen>` icon + version, click calls openFolder()
- `source === 'local'` without sourcePath: `<FolderOpen>` icon + version, disabled

The remove item shows 'Remove' for local plugins and 'Uninstall' for npm plugins (PluginMenu.tsx:49-50).

The destructive remove item has `className="border-border border-t"` (PluginMenu.tsx:83).

Lucide icons render as SVG elements. FolderOpen has a distinctive SVG path. The simplest way to verify the icon is to check that the menuitem does NOT contain a Package icon's known path, OR use a more robust approach: check the SVG's accessible name or a known path segment unique to FolderOpen.

Follow the exact test setup pattern from side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts:
1. Create test config dir with `writeTestConfig()`
2. Start MCP server with `startMcpServer()`
3. Start test server with `startTestServer()`
4. Launch extension with `launchExtensionContext()`
5. Set up adapter symlink with `setupAdapterSymlink()`
6. Wait for extension connected: `waitForExtensionConnected()`
7. Wait for tab sync: `waitForLog(server, 'tab.syncAll received')`
8. Open side panel: `const sidePanelPage = await openSidePanel(context)`
9. Wait for plugin card: `await expect(sidePanelPage.getByText('E2E Test')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 30_000 })`

To open the menu: `sidePanelPage.locator('[aria-label="Plugin options"]').click()`
To find menu items: `sidePanelPage.locator('[role="menuitem"]')`

Constraints:
- Do NOT test click navigation (openFolder calls the MCP server which needs the bridge relay working) — just verify the item is enabled and has the correct icon
- Import the plugin version from package.json like side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts does (getPluginPackageInfo pattern)

userStories.title: E2E tests for browser tools version menu item (local dev server)
userStories.description: Add E2E tests verifying the version item in the browser tools three-dot menu when the server runs from a local dev path (not node_modules). The FolderOpen icon should be shown, the version text should match the server version, and the item should be enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Test opens the three-dot menu on the browser tools card and verifies a menuitem containing 'Server v' and a version string is visible
Test verifies the version menuitem contains a FolderOpen SVG icon (since E2E tests always run the server from the local monorepo, not from node_modules)
Test verifies the version menuitem is not disabled
Test verifies the server version in the menu matches the serverVersion from /health endpoint
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-menu-version.e2e.ts (same file as US-001, add tests to the same describe block)

Context:
The BrowserToolsMenu uses `[aria-label="Browser tools options"]` for its trigger button. The ServerVersionItem in BrowserToolsMenu.tsx renders:
- npm install (serverSourcePath contains 'node_modules'): Package icon, click opens npmjs.com
- local dev (serverSourcePath set, no 'node_modules'): FolderOpen icon, click opens folder
- no serverSourcePath: Package icon, disabled

In E2E tests, the server always runs from the local monorepo (`platform/mcp-server/dist/`), so `serverSourcePath` will be something like `/Users/.../opentabs/platform/mcp-server` — no `node_modules` in path. So the E2E environment will always show the FolderOpen icon for the server.

To verify the server version, fetch it from `GET /health` (the health endpoint returns `{ version: '...' }`). Compare with the version shown in the menu.

The browser tools card is always visible (rendered from BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG). To find the menu button:
```
const browserToolsMenuBtn = sidePanelPage.locator('[aria-label="Browser tools options"]');
```

The menu item will show text like 'Server v0.0.66'. Use a regex like `/Server v\d/` to match.

Constraints:
- Testing the npm branch of ServerVersionItem requires a server installed via npm, which is not easily achievable in E2E. Only test the local dev branch (FolderOpen icon) and add a code comment noting the npm branch is not covered in E2E.
- Reuse the same test file and describe block from US-001. Both plugin and browser tools version tests share the same setup.
